@@ -514,6 +514,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-79ni","title":"Harden all log contract tests across workspace against tracing callsite poisoning","description":"## What
Final verification that the tracing callsite fix is complete, plus minor cleanup.

## Status: 95% DONE — VERIFICATION + TYPO FIX ONLY

### Already Done
- ffs-cli: 6 log contract tests use Dispatch::new + set_default + rebuild_interest_cache
  directly (inline pattern, no helper function). 4 non-log-contract tests serialized via
  log_contract_guard(). EnvFilter removed from test imports.
- ffs-core: 10+ log contract tests use install_json_subscriber() helper.
  Doc comment exists at line 15417-15419 (but has typo: line 15418 says "/ " not "///").
- Zero remaining with_default + EnvFilter in any test code (verified by grep).

### Remaining Work
1. Fix typo in ffs-core install_json_subscriber doc comment (line 15418: "/ " → "///")
2. Run cargo test --workspace 5 consecutive times to prove zero flakes
3. Close bead

## Test Plan
- cargo test --workspace × 5, zero failures

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Doc comment typo fixed
- [ ] 5 consecutive clean test runs
- [ ] Bead closed","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-06T15:50:52.859989786Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-06T22:04:11.511090988Z","closed_at":"2026-04-06T22:04:11.510829654Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-7a45","title":"Verify btrfs RAID56 parity rotation against upstream","description":"Current resolve_raid56_stripe parity rotation in crates/ffs-ondisk/src/btrfs.rs needs validation vs kernel (legacy corpus missing). Confirm rotation formula and adjust tests accordingly.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-13T18:13:51.341820284Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-15T00:42:07.561390886Z","closed_at":"2026-04-15T00:42:07.557440586Z","close_reason":"Verified RAID56 parity rotation is correct. Implementation uses left-symmetric layout: P starts at last device (num-1) and rotates left (decreasing index) each stripe row. Q follows P at adjacent position with wraparound. This was fixed in commit 862b145 and test stripe_resolve_raid6_parity_wraparound validates the behavior.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["btrfs","parity"]}
 {"id":"bd-7ag08","title":"port ext4 move_ext semantic range validation parity","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-20T18:32:23.491108343Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-20T18:40:22.856820293Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T18:40:22.854578962Z","close_reason":"Added ext4 MOVE_EXT semantic range validation parity","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-7awoi","title":"CONFORMANCE: honor FUSE RWF_APPEND write intent","description":"Queue-empty porting-to-rust fallback for pwritev2/io_uring write-intent parity. Implement the currently exposed but unconsumed RWF_APPEND flag in the FUSE write path by resolving the write offset to current EOF under the inode mutation guard, keep RWF_NOAPPEND as the opt-out for O_APPEND-derived append behavior, add focused ffs-fuse unit coverage, and update FEATURE_PARITY.md evidence.","notes":"Claimed by Codex for porting-to-rust parity fallback. Agent Mail health is red due missing schema tables, so coordination is recorded in Beads only.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-25T22:45:48.441241206Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T23:02:24.212845706Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T23:02:24.210982866Z","close_reason":"Implemented FUSE RWF_APPEND/O_APPEND EOF write intent with RWF_NOAPPEND opt-out and updated parity evidence.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-7d2u8","title":"add mounted-path btrfs read-only EROFS coverage for regular file writes","description":"Survey finding: btrfs fuse_e2e exercises writable create and overwrite flows, while ffs-core already returns ReadOnly/EROFS for write on a read-only mount. The mounted-path public refusal contract for ordinary writes is not frozen yet. Scope: add btrfs read-only FUSE probes that attempt create-via-write and overwrite-on-existing-file writes, asserting exact EROFS and unchanged file bytes plus unchanged directory contents. Acceptance: btrfs FUSE E2E freezes operator-visible read-only refusal for regular file writes.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T08:32:34.876214269Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T19:46:38.520232278Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T19:46:38.520232278Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-7lrz","title":"Unblock workspace clippy by fixing ffs-cli struct_field_names and too_many_lines diagnostics","description":"Workspace clippy currently fails in crates/ffs-cli/src/main.rs with: (1) clippy::struct_field_names around line 1544 (all fields share groups postfix), and (2) clippy::too_many_lines around line 1710. Refactor these areas minimally to satisfy -D warnings without behavior changes.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-26T01:43:26.783586997Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-26T01:51:35.377464026Z","closed_at":"2026-02-26T01:51:35.377445572Z","close_reason":"Already fixed — ffs-cli clippy passes clean","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli","clippy","quality"]}
 {"id":"bd-7pde","title":"Pin ioctl metadata-write semantics in facade tests","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PlumCitadel","created_at":"2026-04-01T17:51:23.838976971Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-01T17:55:01.364809680Z","closed_at":"2026-04-01T17:55:01.364493522Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

@@ -285,7 +285,7 @@ behavior of that operation, not whether the row is unimplemented.
 | FUSE readlink | FrankenFS spec §9 | ✅ | `FsOps::readlink` via `OpenFs` |
 | FUSE mount runtime | FrankenFS spec §9 | ✅ | Production runtime lifecycle, signal handling, dispatch coverage, and CI-safe skip behavior are implemented; OQ4 (`bd-h6nz.6.4`) is resolved for V1.x with explicit `flush` (non-durable) vs `fsync`/`fsyncdir` (durability boundary) contract and writeback-cache-disabled policy. `crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs` now emits `SCENARIO_RESULT` coverage for ext4 (`ext4_rw_flush`, `ext4_rw_fdatasync`, `ext4_rw_fsync`, `ext4_rw_fsyncdir`, `ext4_rw_seek_data_hole`, `ext4_rw_seek_hole_fully_allocated`, `ext4_rw_seek_leading_hole`, `ext4_rw_seek_all_hole`) and btrfs (`btrfs_rw_flush`, `btrfs_rw_fdatasync`, `btrfs_rw_fsync`, `btrfs_rw_fsyncdir`, `btrfs_rw_seek_data_hole`, `btrfs_rw_seek_hole_fully_allocated`, `btrfs_rw_seek_leading_hole`, `btrfs_rw_seek_all_hole`) through real file flushes, file- and directory-FD sync calls, and mounted-path `SEEK_DATA`/`SEEK_HOLE` probes for punched-hole, fully allocated, leading-hole, and all-hole layouts with EOF `ENXIO` verification; btrfs read-only mounted-path `fsync`, `fdatasync`, and `fsyncdir` now also freeze exact `EROFS` with no data or dirent drift. |
 | FUSE ABI 7.40 protocol surface | FUSE kernel ABI 7.40 / libfuse 3.x | ✅ | Workspace builds the vendored `fuser` crate with `abi-7-40`, and `FrankenFuse::init` now negotiates `FUSE_SPLICE_READ`, `FUSE_SPLICE_WRITE`, `FUSE_SPLICE_MOVE`, and `FUSE_PASSTHROUGH` with stack depth 1 when the kernel advertises support. The vendored `fuser` protocol layer now exposes opcode 52 (`FUSE_STATX`), parses `fuse_statx_in`, replies with Linux statx-compatible `fuse_statx_out`, and preserves `FileAttr::crtime` as `STATX_BTIME`; `FrankenFuse::statx` routes that opcode through the existing `getattr` backend. The vendored ABI surface also exposes `FUSE_WRITE_KILL_SUIDGID`/legacy `FUSE_WRITE_KILL_PRIV` plus Linux `RWF_*` constants for write-intent propagation. Unit coverage freezes ABI constants, STATX request parsing, birth-time reply encoding, and mounted splice pipe-to-file transfer. |
-| FUSE pwritev2/io_uring RWF write intent propagation | `pwritev2(2)`, FUSE `WRITE` request flags | ✅ | `FrankenFuse::write` now decodes synchronous write intent from the FUSE `flags` field (`O_DSYNC`/`O_SYNC`) and routes it through `FsOps::fsync` after the normal MVCC commit, so `RWF_DSYNC` uses a data-sync boundary and `RWF_SYNC` uses a full sync boundary. The implementation deliberately does not treat `fuse_write_in.write_flags` bit 1 as `RWF_DSYNC`, because that bit is already `FUSE_WRITE_LOCKOWNER`; unit coverage freezes that collision and verifies no accidental fsync. `RWF_NOWAIT` uses the non-conflicting RWF bit to attempt inode mutation-lock acquisition without blocking and returns `EAGAIN` when the lock is already held. `crates/ffs-fuse/src/lib.rs` unit tests cover DSync, Sync, collision, and NOWAIT fast-fail behavior, while `crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs` adds mounted-path `os.pwritev(...)` probes for both `RWF_DSYNC` and `RWF_SYNC`, proving requested bytes become immediately readable and survive remount when the host permits FUSE. |
+| FUSE pwritev2/io_uring RWF write intent propagation | `pwritev2(2)`, FUSE `WRITE` request flags | ✅ | `FrankenFuse::write` now decodes synchronous write intent from the FUSE `flags` field (`O_DSYNC`/`O_SYNC`) and routes it through `FsOps::fsync` after the normal MVCC commit, so `RWF_DSYNC` uses a data-sync boundary and `RWF_SYNC` uses a full sync boundary. The implementation deliberately does not treat `fuse_write_in.write_flags` bit 1 as `RWF_DSYNC`, because that bit is already `FUSE_WRITE_LOCKOWNER`; unit coverage freezes that collision and verifies no accidental fsync. `RWF_NOWAIT` uses the non-conflicting RWF bit to attempt inode mutation-lock acquisition without blocking and returns `EAGAIN` when the lock is already held. `RWF_APPEND` and `O_APPEND` resolve the write offset to current EOF under the inode mutation guard, while `RWF_NOAPPEND` preserves the caller offset on an append-open file. `crates/ffs-fuse/src/lib.rs` unit tests cover DSync, Sync, collision, NOWAIT fast-fail, append, and NOAPPEND behavior, while `crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs` adds mounted-path `os.pwritev(...)` probes for both `RWF_DSYNC` and `RWF_SYNC`, proving requested bytes become immediately readable and survive remount when the host permits FUSE. |
 | FUSE inotify rename + dentry notification lifecycle | `fs/notify/fsnotify.c`, `fs/notify/inotify/inotify_user.c`, FUSE notify protocol | ✅ | `FrankenFuse` captures a `fuser::Notifier` during blocking, background, and managed mount setup, then issues `FUSE_NOTIFY_INVAL_ENTRY` for successfully committed `unlink`, `rmdir`, and both old/new directory entries after `rename`. Plain renames deliberately avoid `FUSE_NOTIFY_DELETE`, preserving the kernel/VFS `IN_MOVED_FROM` + `IN_MOVED_TO` cookie-pair contract instead of synthesizing delete/create observations. `crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs` now carries mounted-path inotify probes for 100 same-parent renames and a cross-directory rename; both assert non-zero matching cookies and reject `IN_CREATE`, `IN_DELETE`, `IN_Q_OVERFLOW`, and `IN_IGNORED` during plain rename bursts, with the standard CI soft-skip when the current host denies FUSE mounting. |
 | FUSE ioctl FIEMAP | `include/linux/fiemap.h` | ✅ | `FsOps::fiemap` queries ext4 extent tree via `collect_extents_with_scope` and btrfs extent data items via `btrfs_fiemap_extent_items`. FUSE `ioctl` handler parses `FS_IOC_FIEMAP` (0xC020660B), rejects unsupported request flags with `EBADR`, honors `FIEMAP_FLAG_SYNC` by fsyncing writable mounts before extent lookup, and marshals the fiemap header plus extent array. `crates/ffs-core` now has deterministic btrfs coverage for inline extents (`physical=0`, `FIEMAP_EXTENT_LAST`) and keep-size prealloc extents (`FIEMAP_EXTENT_UNWRITTEN`), while `crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs` verifies mounted-path FIEMAP reporting for ext4 regular files, ext4 `SYNC` and invalid-request-flag contracts, plus btrfs regular, inline, and keep-size prealloc files. Note: FUSE transport may not deliver this ioctl to userspace on all kernel versions. |
 | FUSE ioctl EXT4_IOC_GETFLAGS | `fs/ext4/ioctl.c` | ✅ | `FsOps::get_inode_flags` returns raw ext4 `i_flags` field. FUSE `ioctl` handler dispatches `EXT4_IOC_GETFLAGS` (0x80086601). Harness integration coverage in `crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs` now verifies mounted-path `GETFLAGS` on a writable ext4 file. |
